content: read reviews/1030.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/1030.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/1030.md.

    36‚Üí- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.

    46‚Üí- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5]`.
    47‚Üí- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).

    51‚Üí
    52‚ÜíThe problem is stated as a direct assertion ‚Äî "prove the existence of some c > 0 such that lim R(k+1,k)/R(k,k) > 1+c" ‚Äî so **Case 3** applies.

    82‚Üí

    84‚Üí

    86‚Üí- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`‚àÉ`, `‚àÄ`, `‚â•`, `‚Ñï`, `‚Ñù`, `‚ÇÄ`).
    87‚Üí- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).

    89‚Üí- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all <100 characters).
    90‚Üí- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    91‚Üí

    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review ‚Äî must be verified separately.
    95‚Üí- [ ] All five custom linters pass ‚Äî must be verified separately.
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí---
    98‚Üí
    99‚Üí**Summary:** All checkable items pass. The file is clean, well-structured, and correctly formalizes Erd≈ës Problem 1030 as a direct assertion (Case 3) with no `answer()` wrapper. The `ramseyR` definition is properly docstringed, marked `noncomputable`, and uses `sInf` over the set of graph sizes witnessing the Ramsey property. The theorem statement faithfully captures "‚àÉ c > 0 such that eventually R(k+1,k)/R(k,k) ‚â• 1+c" via an Œµ-K‚ÇÄ formulation, which is equivalent to the original limit statement (the existential quantifier over c makes the ‚â• vs > distinction immaterial). The docstring is transparent about this formalization choice. Build verification remains to be confirmed.

file.content:
# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist ‚Äî `deepmind/1030.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd≈ës Problem 1030`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1030](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1030)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (existence of c > 0 such that lim R(k+1,k)/R(k,k) > 1+c).
- [x] Bibliography entry for related paper: `[Er93]`.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside a namespace: `namespace Erdos1030 ... end Erdos1030`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos1030`.
- [x] `open` statement appears **before** the namespace block: `open SimpleGraph` on line 35.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] Helper `def` `ramseyR` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Definition is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`, a classical operation).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Function returning a type uses `lowerCamelCase`: `ramseyR`.
- N/A: No `local notation` used (none needed).
- N/A: No `@[category test]` / `@[category API]` sanity checks added (optional "consider" item).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Benchmark problem uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_1030` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Theorem docstring references source paper: `[Er93, p. 339]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem is stated as a direct assertion ‚Äî "prove the existence of some c > 0 such that lim R(k+1,k)/R(k,k) > 1+c" ‚Äî so **Case 3** applies.

- [x] The theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper). Correct for a direct assertion ("prove that...").
- N/A: Cases 1 and 2 do not apply.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research open` ‚Äî a valid category.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute (not multiple separate tags).
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5` (Combinatorics).
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order (single entry ‚Äî trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjecture uses multi-line `by` / `sorry` format (`:= by\n  sorry`), which is acceptable.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- N/A: File is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. The problem is a single assertion.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`‚àÉ`, `‚àÄ`, `‚â•`, `‚Ñï`, `‚Ñù`, `‚ÇÄ`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all <100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review ‚Äî must be verified separately.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass ‚Äî must be verified separately.

---

**Summary:** All checkable items pass. The file is clean, well-structured, and correctly formalizes Erd≈ës Problem 1030 as a direct assertion (Case 3) with no `answer()` wrapper. The `ramseyR` definition is properly docstringed, marked `noncomputable`, and uses `sInf` over the set of graph sizes witnessing the Ramsey property. The theorem statement faithfully captures "‚àÉ c > 0 such that eventually R(k+1,k)/R(k,k) ‚â• 1+c" via an Œµ-K‚ÇÄ formulation, which is equivalent to the original limit statement (the existential quantifier over c makes the ‚â• vs > distinction immaterial). The docstring is transparent about this formalization choice. Build verification remains to be confirmed.

content.text: The review passed ‚Äî all checkable items have checkmarks, and the summary confirms everything is clean. No changes needed.
